title: Encoding of Sound Source Elevation in Human Cortex
description: This dataset comprises neuroimaging recordings from two experiments investigating the cortical encoding of sound source elevation in human listeners. Subjects were presented with noise bursts from loudspeakers positioned at various elevations while undergoing behavioral testing and neural recording. The dataset includes data from a deviant detection task (Experiment 1) and a one-back task (Experiment 2), with accompanying localization test results and behavioral responses.
methods_description: Experiments were conducted in a hemi-anechoic chamber. Subjects were presented with bursts of pink or white noise from loudspeakers at different elevations and 10° azimuth. Experiment 1 (deviant detection) involved adapter-probe stimulus pairs at varying elevations; Experiment 2 (one-back) presented noise bursts through open headphones followed by loudspeaker stimuli. Behavioral responses were recorded via button press within one-second windows for deviant detection and via localization responses for target trials.
